criterion_ref: Requirements 1-2; Constraints: exact raw request bytes
message: Raw-body capture only runs when express.json() recognizes the Content-Type. Live probes with a correct HMAC and either Content-Type: text/plain or no Content-Type returned 401 invalid_signature because req.rawBody was never set, so the signature is not actually checked before body validation across these input classes.

criterion_ref: Out of Scope: existing GET routes — no behavior changes
message: The webhook-specific JSON-parse error handler is registered globally via app.use(), not scoped to /webhook, so it intercepts malformed-JSON bodies sent to ANY route including the pre-existing GET routes. A live GET /health probe with a malformed-JSON body and Content-Type: application/json returned 401 invalid_signature; baseline (pre-diff) behavior for the same request was Express's generic 400 HTML parse-error page. This changes observable behavior for existing GET routes, violating the spec's explicit Out of Scope constraint ('No changes to ... existing GET /health, GET /items, GET /items/:id behavior') and the goal's 'Only touch server/index.js and tests/server.test.js' framing (the file changed, but the behavioral surface leaked beyond the new endpoint).

criterion_ref: Requirement 2: data an object; invalid shape returns 400 invalid_body
message: The validator accepts arrays for data because typeof [] is 'object'. A live correctly signed request with data: [] returned 200 and was tracked as accepted, although a JSON array is not the required JSON object. Note: this was an explicit, reasoned PLAN interpretation (spec says 'an object' with no stated array exclusion; adding one would be unrequested speculative validation) rather than an oversight.

criterion_ref: Verification: npm test exits 0 and stdout contains # fail 0
message: The required npm test verification could not be established in this judge's own sandbox: all server tests failed when listen(0) raised EPERM. Direct app.handle probes exercised the GET routes and webhook interactions, but this judge sandbox cannot itself prove the mandated npm-test exit code and '# fail 0' output (the orchestrator's independent MECHANICAL run and BUILD_GATE already established this; this is an environment note, not a code defect).
